_meta.keyKnowledge: We learn what we can understand from intercultural experiences, including developing a respectful and critical perspective on different cultures, including our own.

_source.w: We learn what we can understand from intercultural experiences, including developing a respectful and critical perspective on different cultures, including our own.
y: Intercultural experiences teach us things we cannot learn from books alone.
A critical perspective means we can appreciate cultures while also thinking carefully about practices.
Learning to see our own culture from the outside develops self-awareness and humility.
eg: We reflect on what we learned from an intercultural experience — a visit, a guest speaker, a cultural event.
We discuss how meeting someone from a different culture changed how we think.
We practise looking at our own culture from an outsider's perspective — what might seem unusual?
We explore how stereotypes can prevent us from truly understanding other cultures.
We discuss the difference between appreciating a culture and appropriating it.
We develop respectful questions we could ask to learn more about someone else's culture.
